text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data, which includes input examples and their correct outputs. Using this data, it builds a model — a kind of mathematical rule for making decisions. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, after training on thousands of labeled emails, a model can predict whether a new email is spam. The accuracy of these predictions shows how well the model has learned. The more high-quality training data it receives, the better its performance becomes.
